schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

schemas.name: Form 15CA/15CB — Foreign Remittances
schemas.description: Form 15CA and 15CB filing for foreign remittances. CA certificate for cross-border payments. DTAA benefit claims. FEMA compliance. Virtual Auditor.

schemas.description: Form 15CA and 15CB filing for foreign remittances. CA certificate for cross-border payments. DTAA benefit claims. FEMA compliance. Virtual Auditor.

schemas.name: Form 15CA / 15CB Filing
schemas.description: Form 15CA and 15CB filing for foreign remittances. CA certificate for cross-border payments. DTAA benefit claims. FEMA compliance. Virtual Auditor.

step.text: Determine Part (A/B/C/D) based on remittance type
step.name: Step 1

step.text: CA examines DTAA applicability and TDS compliance
step.name: Step 2

mainEntity.name: Is 15CB needed for all foreign payments?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Only for Part C remittances (taxable payments exceeding \u20b95 lakhs not covered by RBI general permission). Imports of goods and certain specified payments require only 15CA Part A or B.

Double Taxation Avoidance Agreements between India and other countries may provide lower tax rates or exemptions for specific types of income (royalties, FTS, dividends, interest). We analyse the applicable DTAA and claim benefits in Form 15CB to reduce withholding tax.

Standard: 2-3 working days. Express (urgent payment): same-day for straightforward remittances. Requires: invoice, agreement, PAN of payee (if available), TRC (Tax Residency Certificate) for DTAA claims.

Required for all foreign remittances (except specified exemptions like imports, embassy payments, student fees up to threshold). 15CA is the remittance information form. 15CB is the CA certificate certifying taxability and applicable DTAA.

A practicing Chartered Accountant (not the assessee themselves even if they are a CA). The CA certifies the nature of remittance, applicable TDS rate, DTAA benefit claimed, and tax deposited. It is a professional certification under Section 195(6).

mainEntity.name: What are the different parts of Form 15CA?

acceptedAnswer.text: Part A: remittance below ₹5 lakhs (no 15CB needed). Part B: remittance covered under Section 195(2)/195(3)/197 order. Part C: remittance where 15CB is required (above ₹5 lakhs, taxable). Part D: remittance not taxable under IT Act.

mainEntity.name: What is the penalty for not filing 15CA/15CB?

acceptedAnswer.text: Bank cannot process the remittance without 15CA acknowledgment. Penalty under Section 271-I: ₹1 lakh for failure to furnish information. Tax demand on remitter if TDS obligation not complied with.

CA can issue 15CB within 1-2 working days if documents are ready. 15CA filed online on Income Tax portal — instant acknowledgment. Bank processes remittance within 1-2 days of receiving 15CA acknowledgment number.
